text:
Once upon a time, in a small, cozy village nestled among whispering woods and tranquil streams, there lived a curious pigeon named Polly. Polly was unlike the others; her feathers were a striking blue, and she had a small, wide-head perched atop her feathered crown. Despite her vibrant plumage, Polly lived the humblest life a bird could with no fancy feathers, no glitter, and barely any flight ability besides a feeble flap that often sent her straight back to the spot she started from.

Polly's everyday life was simple: waking up in her favorite hollow just below the ancient oak tree at the village's edge, eating seeds she’d collected during breakfast, and watching the world go by. She was particularly fascinated by the odd creatures that dared to enter her world—such as the farmer's oxen and curious cats—never daring to dive too closely, keeping their distance with polite meows.
